title: Cacalia gochnatioides
intro: Cacalia gochnatioides, also known as Mexican waterleaf or Huazontle, is a perennial plant species native to Mexico and parts of Central America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Cacalia gochnatioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cacalia gochnatioides is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion to treat various health conditions, and its leaves are also used in salads and as a cooked vegetable.

The plant's classification also places it within the family Asteraceae, which is a larger family of flowering plants that includes daisies and sunflowers. Cacalia gochnatioides is a member of the order Asterales and is native to certain regions of the world.
